heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 225 and it happened on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 2nd among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 19 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwave.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (204°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 9.1°C) and outside temperature (down 7.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 21%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 28291 lux) and cloud levels (up 2676 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 25854 lux), cloud levels (up 1880 m), and outside humidity (down 8%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2680m lower than yesterday, 1880.0m lower than the past week, 1190.8m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1974m minimum and 8460m maximum, with an average of 2510m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4390m and 3700m.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; you can expect comfortable, mild temperatures. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and very little to no wind will be present. Drizzle or light rain possible, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
